findings.evidence: `rg -n "isComplexLinear_arrowCongr_iff" code/TauCeti` shows the raw public lemma only at `Transport.lean:124`, used only to prove `isComplexLinearMap_arrowCongr_iff` at lines 139-146.
findings.fix: Make this helper `private`, or move it to `TauCeti.LinearAlgebra.ComplexLinearPart` with its own intended public API and annotations.
findings.issue: `isComplexLinear_arrowCongr_iff` exposes a raw `IsComplexLinear` helper from the symplectic transport file even though the advertised downstream API is the bundled `AlmostComplexStructure` transport.

findings.evidence: `Transport.lean:139` declares `lemma isComplexLinearMap_arrowCongr_iff` with no attribute, while the underlying raw definitional API is automation-tagged in `ComplexLinearPart.lean:79` as `@[simp, grind =] theorem isComplexLinear_iff`.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to `isComplexLinearMap_arrowCongr_iff`; if the raw helper remains public, give it the same normal-form annotation as well.
findings.issue: The characteristic equivalence `isComplexLinearMap_arrowCongr_iff` is not tagged for simplification, so `simp` will not normalize transported complex-linearity goals back to the original map.

summary: The bundled transport API has the main characteristic lemmas, but the public surface still exposes a raw helper outside the named downstream target and leaves the transported-linearity equivalence out of automation.
